summary:
Geniculate neuralgia involves the geniculate ganglion, which is part of cranial nerve VII—the facial nerve. This condition arises due to irritation or compression affecting sensation from areas such as the anterior two-thirds of the tongue and ear.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$
